finding.summary: Audio has digital clipping (sample peak 0.2 dBFS) and excessive true peak (0.8 dBTP).
finding.probable_cause: Input gain too high during recording; no headroom left.
finding.confidence_reason: Sample peak above 0 dBFS and true peak 0.8 dBTP are direct measurements of clipping. Silence fraction is low (0.05), so levels are representative.

evidence_refs.description: Sample peak is 0.2 dBFS, indicating digital clipping.
True peak is 0.8 dBTP, exceeding 0 dBTP, confirming intersample overs.

proposal.reason: Preview changing track volume from 0.000 dB to -3.000 dB to test whether true_peak_db decreases.
